Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

SpTU 5, 291 [sale of field][via cams/gkab]

Obverse
o 1o 1

A.ŠA₃ KIRI₆ gišGIŠIMMAR ša₂ <<KU₃.BABBAR>> <ina> muḫ-ḫi id₂ḫar-ra

(o 1) The field, garden of date palms on the Harru canal, in the middle of the city of Uruk, adjacent to the field, garden of the ground of the Lady of Uruk, (field) of Eriba, son of Kinaya, descendant of Gimil-Nanaya, the price of (this) field and 2 ... and [...] is not enough.

o 22

ina MURU₂ IRI ša₂ UNUGki US₂.SA.DU <<x x>>

o 33

A.ŠA₃ KIRI₆ ina KUR ša₂ dGAŠAN—ša₂—UNUGki ša₂

o 44

meri-ba A-šu₂ ⸢ša₂ m⸣ki-na-a A mŠU—dna-na-a

o 55

ma-ḫir⸢ir⸣ [A].ŠA₃ u₃ 2 ina še-ri u₃

o 66

x [...] ⸢u₃⸣ ma-ṭu meri-ba A-šu₂ ša₂

(o 6) Eriba, son of Kinaya, Descendant of Gimil-Nanaya has agreed a price with Nidintu, son of Ina-ṣilli-Taštimmu, descendant of [PN] and Turemenitu, his wife, daughter of Kinaya for the 1/2 mina of block silver and they! bought the field (at) the full price (for a) total of 1/2 mina 1/2 shekel, including 1/2 shekel they gave as additional amount. [...]

o 77

mki-⸢na-a A⸣ m⸢ŠU⸣—dna-na-a ki-i 1/2 MA.NA KU₃.BABBAR KU₃.[PAD.DU]

o 88

it-ti mni-din-tu₄ A-šu₂ ša₂ mina—GISSU—dtaš-tim-mu A [m...]

o 99

u₃ ftu-reme-ni-tu₄ DAM-šu₂ DUMU.MUNUS ša₂ mki-⸢na⸣-[a]

o 1010

GANBA im-be₂-e-ma i-ša₂-am ši-i-mu A.⸢ŠA₃⸣

o 1111

gam-ru-tu ŠU.NIGIN 1/2 MA.NA 1/2 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR KU₃.⸢PAD⸣.[DU]

o 1212

a-di 1/2 GIN₂ KU₃.BABBAR ša₂ ki-i a-tar id-di-⸢nu⸣ [...]

o 1313

mni-din-tu₄ A-šu₂ ša₂ mina—GISSU—dtaš-tim-mu ⸢A⸣ [m...]

(o 13) Nidintu, son of Ina-ṣilli-Taštimmu, descendant of [PN], and Turemenitu, his wife (gave) the price [... (for)] their fields. The complete price was received [... in the] hand of Eriba, son of Kinaya, descendant of [Gimil-Nanaya], satisfaction was given, there is no more [liable] to claim [...]. They will not come back. Mutually [...]. Every time the brothers, the sons, the family [...will bring an action against] the house of Nidintu and Turemenitu, [his wife], concerning this field like this: "the silver was not paid [...]." ... the silver [...] they will satisfy 12 times [...] of silver, price of the field [... PN], daughter of Anu-... [...] Adad-ibni, her husband [...] at the sealing of the clay tablet [...].

o 1414

u₃ ftu-reme-ni-tu₄ DAM-šu₂ ši-i-⸢mu⸣ [...]

o 1515

A.ŠA₃-šu₂-nu ka-sap ga-mer-ti [... ina?]

o 1616

qa-at meri-ba A-šu₂ ša₂ mki-na-a A m[ŠU—dna-na-a]

o 1717

ma-ḫi-ir a-pil ru-gum₂-ma-a ⸢ul⸣ [...]

o 1818

ul i-tur-ru-u₂ a-na a-ḫa-miš [...]

o 1919

ma-ti-ma ŠEŠ-MEŠ DUMU-MEŠ ⸢IM⸣.[RI.A ...]

o 2020

E₂ mni-din-tu₄ u₃ ftu-⸢reme⸣-[ni-tu₄ DAM-šu₂]

o 2121

ša₂ a-na muḫ-ḫi A.ŠA₃ šu-a-ti₃ [i-dab-bu-bu ...]

Bottom
b.e. 1b.e. 1

⸢um⸣-ma A.ŠA₃ šu-u₂-a-ti₃ ⸢ul⸣ [na-din-ma ...]

kas₃-pi ul ma-ḫi-⸢ir⸣ [...]

mdIŠKUR—DU₃ DAM-⸢šu₂⸣ [...]

r 77

ina ka-⸢nak⸣ im⸢DUB⸣ [...]

r 88

lu₂IGI mNA₃—A—MU A-šu₂ ša₂ [m...]

(r 8) Witnesses: Nabu-apla-iddin, son of [PN], descendant of Eriba; [PN, son of PN], descendant of Gimil-Nanaya; [PN], son of Nabu-šum-ukin, [descendant of PN]; Šamaš-mukin-apli, son of ...-ibni, descendant of Kidin-DN; Šamaš-mukin-apli, son of Bel-nadin-apla, descendant of [PN]; Bel-..., son of Bel-nadin-apla, descendant of [...]-Nanaya; Anu-ah-iddin, son of Laqipu, descendant of of Sin-leqi-unninni; Gimillu, son of Marduk-nadin-aha, descendant of Nannariya.

r 99

A meri-ba x [...]

r 1010

A mŠU—dna-na-a mx-[...]

r 1111

A-šu₂ ša₂ mNA₃!—MU—DU [A m...]-⸢ri?⸣-i

mdUTU—DU—A A-šu₂ ša₂ ⸢m⸣[...].⸢DU₃?⸣

A mki-din-dx [...] mdUTU—DU—A A-šu₂ ša₂

r 1414

mdEN—MU—A ⸢A?⸣ [m...]-PA-DI ⸢m?⸣d⸢EN?⸣-RA-TAR A-šu₂ ša₂

r 1515

mdEN—MU—A ⸢A?⸣ [mx-x-(x)—d]⸢na⸣-na-a-<<a>> mda-nu—ŠEŠ—MU

r 1616

A-šu₂ ša₂ mla-qi₂-⸢pu? A⸣ md30—TI—ER₂

r 1717

mgi-mil-lu A-šu₂ ša₂ mdAMAR.UTU—MU—ŠEŠ

r 1818

A mna-an-na-ri-a ⸢m⸣NIG₂.BA—dAMAR.UTU lu₂UMBISAG

(r 18) Qišti-Marduk, the scribe, son of Marduk-apla-iddin, descendant of Šiguʾa. At Uruk, the month Kislimu, 5th day, 12th year of Darius, king of Babylon, king of [all the lands].

r 1919

A-šu₂ ša₂ mdAMAR.UTU—A—⸢MU?⸣ A mši-gu-u₂-a

r 2020

UNUGki itiGAN.⸢GAN U₄⸣ 5-KAM₂ MU 12-KAM₂

Top
t.e. 1t.e. 1

mda-a-ri-ia-a-muš LUGAL TIN.TIRki LUGAL [KUR.KUR]

t.e. 22

ṣu-pur mni-din-tu₄ u₃

(t.e. 2) Nails of Nidintu and Turemenitu, the seller of the prebend, like their seals.

t.e. 33

ftu-reme-ni-tu₄ ⸢na-din-ne₂-e⸣

t.e. 44

GIŠ.ŠUB.BA ki-ma imKIŠIB-šu-nu

6 nail impressions, 3 on the right and 3 on the left
